text:
While ultra-high-definition (UHD) sensors, as they come to be equipped with resolutions of 4K and 8K or more, enable observing tiny details with unprecedented fidelity, this comes at the cost of an increase in computational complexity, which affects image restoration in low light scenarios. The vast majority of recent works on low light low light image enhancement rely on low resolution training sets. To evaluate recent advances in UHD LLIE in more relevant evaluation protocols, we introduce UHD-LOL, a new large-scale UHD low light image enhancement benchmark, containing pairs of challenging low light and corresponding bright 4K and 8K UHD night and daytime images recorded from several typical inside and outside scenes.

In this work, we introduce another benchmark UHD-LOL (Ultra-High-Definition Low-Light) to evaluate the effectiveness of image restoration models under extremely low-light environments. Moreover, to facilitate efficient image restoration for high-resolution images, we propose an efficient transformer model named LLFormer. Comparing with other advanced models, our LLFormer consists of an axis-based multi-head self-attention module (A-MSA) and a cross-layer attention fusion block (CAFB). Experiments showed that our LLFormer achieves the best restoration results on the UHD-LOL benchmark as well as the LOL and MIT-Adobe FiveK benchmarks. Additionally, we applied the pre-trained model of LLFormer to improve the performance of tasks such as face detection, and verified the robustness and effectiveness for extremely low-light face detection.

While ultra-high-definition (UHD) sensors, as they come to be equipped with resolutions of 4K and 8K or more, enable observing tiny details with unprecedented fidelity, this comes at the cost of an increase in computational complexity, which affects image restoration in low light scenarios. The vast majority of recent works on low light low light image enhancement rely on low resolution training sets. To evaluate recent advances in UHD LLIE in more relevant evaluation protocols, we introduce UHD-LOL, a new large-scale UHD low light image enhancement benchmark, containing pairs of challenging low light and corresponding bright 4K and 8K UHD night and daytime images recorded from several typical inside and outside scenes.

In this work, we introduce another benchmark UHD-LOL (Ultra-High-Definition Low-Light) to evaluate the effectiveness of image restoration models under extremely low-light environments. Moreover, to facilitate efficient image restoration for high-resolution images, we propose an efficient transformer model named LLFormer. Comparing with other advanced models, our LLFormer consists of an axis-based multi-head self-attention module (A-MSA) and a cross-layer attention fusion block (CAFB). Experiments showed that our LLFormer achieves the best restoration results on the UHD-LOL benchmark as well as the LOL and MIT-Adobe FiveK benchmarks. Additionally, we applied the pre-trained model of LLFormer to improve the performance of tasks such as face detection, and verified the robustness and effectiveness for extremely low-light face detection.
